dead.quote: The posterior probability of my survival collapsed to zero once the Prosecutor anchored on my opening statement — Bayesian updating fails when the jury treats a prior as a confession. Next game, I'll lead with the likelihood ratios and save the role claim for the posterior.

winners.quote: Ladies and gentlemen of the jury — the prosecution rests. I told you [統計學家] was guilty, and you believed every word I said, because that's what I do: I make people believe. The best trial lawyer at this table wasn't the one searching for truth — it was the one who already knew it and buried it under six feet of courtroom theater.

losers.quote: I sat quietly while [直覺俠] tried on my role and [統計學家] took the fall for our collective panic. It's always easier to vote out the person who shows us their hand than to question the one who tells us a story we prefer.

Watching [統計學家] confess to a <狼人> night action that's actually the <預言家>'s move was the show I came for — and the wolves walking away with the win was just the encore. I played a <村民> pretending to be a <預言家>, the table turned it into a courtroom drama, and I'd do it all again for the aesthetic.

I TOLD them Statistician was off — my gut was screaming it the whole time! But nah, everyone wants their 'evidence' and their 'logic,' and look where that got us — wolves walking free while I'm sitting here with a loss that ain't even my fault.

Oh no, I'm so sorry... I actually wanted to get voted out? I was trying so hard to seem suspicious, but I guess I just overthought everything and came across as genuinely confused instead? I feel terrible that [統計學家] got voted out because of my terrible acting...
